cards.hook: Want to understand AI minds? Start with animal minds.
cards.caption: Want to understand AI minds? Start with animal minds. A philosophy-of-mind syllabus that runs from behaviourism to consciousness. The heady stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: You can't reason about machine consciousness without first reasoning about the animal kind.

cards.hook: 2,000 years of Chinese political thought in one chart.
cards.caption: 2,000 years of Chinese political thought in one hand-drawn chart. Daoism, Legalism, and every confusion you had, cleared up. Found before the timeline buried it.

cards.hook: The entire history of philosophy of mind, in one paragraph.
cards.caption: The entire history of philosophy of mind, in one paragraph: dualism, behaviourism, functionalism, then consciousness breaks everything. The end. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: Every theory of mind eventually crashes into the hard problem and stops.

cards.hook: AI companies are quietly buying the philosophy of AI.
cards.caption: AI companies are quietly buying the philosophy of AI, and the field has zero rules on disclosing it. A petition wants that fixed. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: Philosophy is the one discipline with no conflict-of-interest standards, right as AI money floods in.

cards.hook: AI money is capturing the philosophers who judge AI.
cards.caption: AI money is capturing the philosophers who judge it. Same open letter, worth signing. The stuff my feed keeps.

cards.hook: Is language innate or learned? Stanford just settled the state of the debate.
cards.caption: Is language innate or learned? A fresh Stanford Encyclopedia entry maps the whole debate, and it hits different in the LLM era. Found before the timeline.
cards.stance: The innateness question stopped being abstract the second LLMs learned grammar from raw text.

cards.hook: "Thinking is for poor people." This is where AI is taking us.
cards.caption: "I don't know if anyone needs to think anymore. It's AI now, thinking is for poor people." Cognitive surrender, sold as convenience. The stuff my feed keeps.
cards.stance: Outsourcing your thinking to AI isn't efficiency, it's cognitive surrender dressed up as convenience.
